product.one_line_description: Higgsfield CLI enables AI agents to generate images and videos, train characters, analyze videos, and create marketing content by connecting to the Higgsfield platform via the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

authentication.auth_summary: Authentication for the Higgsfield CLI is performed using the `higgsfield auth login` command, which opens a browser for a user to log in with their Higgsfield account. The platform explicitly states that no API keys are required or used for authentication.

developer_access.access_notes: Developers can install the Higgsfield CLI via `npm install -g @higgsfield/cli`. Access is then gained by running `higgsfield auth login`, which initiates a browser-based login process using an existing Higgsfield account. The system uses a credit system tied to existing Higgsfield plans, implying a paid model for usage, but the initial CLI access and authentication process do not require human approval.

api_surface.api_summary: The primary programmatic interface is the Higgsfield CLI, which allows AI agents to leverage a broad range of capabilities including image and video generation (up to 4K resolution and 15 seconds respectively), character training, video analysis, marketing video creation, viral clip generation, and virality prediction. It connects to over 30 AI models and supports iterative workflows.

mcp.mcp_summary: Higgsfield officially supports the Model Context Protocol (MCP), an open standard for AI agents to access external tools. Agents such as Claude, OpenClaw, and Hermes can connect to Higgsfield by adding the Higgsfield MCP server URL in their settings and authenticating via a Higgsfield account. MCP enables agents to generate content, train characters, and browse creation history.

buildability.verdict_reasoning: Application is technically buildable, subject to specific account or tier constraints.

Higgsfield uses MCP (Model Context Protocol), an open standard that gives AI agents access to external tools. Once connected, your agent can generate images, create videos, train characters, and browse your creation history, all within a single session.

evidence_pool.claim: The CLI and MCP enable AI agents to access a broad range of AI capabilities including image and video generation, character training, video analysis, marketing video creation, viral clip generation, and virality prediction, leveraging 30+ underlying AI models.
evidence_pool.source_title: Higgsfield CLI | AI Image & Video Generation for Any Agent
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet:
Generate, Product Photoshoot & Soul ID
Video analyzer
Marketing video generator
Soul character training
Cinematic image to video
Viral clip generator
Virality prediction
Higgsfield gives your agent access to 30+ models including Soul, Cinema Studio, Flux, Seedream, Kling, Minimax Hailuo, Veo, and more. Your agent automatically selects the best model for the task, or you can specify one yourself.
Images up to 4K resolution, videos up to 15 seconds across multiple cinematic styles, consistent characters using Soul training, and more. You can generate from text prompts, reference images, or a combination of both.

evidence_pool.source_title: Higgsfield CLI | AI Image & Video Generation for Any Agent
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet:
How does Higgsfield connect to AI agents?
Higgsfield uses MCP (Model Context Protocol), an open standard that gives AI agents access to external tools.
Which agents are supported?
Higgsfield MCP works with Claude (web, Cowork, and Claude Code), OpenClaw, Hermes Agent, and NemoClaw. Any agent or client that supports MCP can connect to Higgsfield.

evidence_pool.claim: Higgsfield officially supports the Model Context Protocol (MCP), an open standard that allows AI agents like Claude, OpenClaw, and Hermes to access Higgsfield's tools for image and video generation, character training, and more. Users add the Higgsfield MCP server URL in their agent's settings for connection.
